attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches late in chaotic finales, demonstrating superior bike handling and positioning instincts. Excels in bike-throw finishes after messy lead-outs disintegrate.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on Groupama-FDJ leadout train delivering him to final 200m; struggles when teammates are shed early or positioning gets disrupted by rival teams. UAE also provided perfect leadout in 2020.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages, but notably capable on hot, hilly parcours up to 150km. Survives brutal mountain stages to contest third-week sprints, showing exceptional recovery capacity for a pure sprinter.
vulnerability: Positioning errors when team support fails early in race; solo positioning strategy collapses without leadout train. Vulnerable when forced to follow others rather than trusting team structure.
key_patterns: Multiple stage wins within single Grand Tour: 3 wins in 12 stages (Giro 2022), 4 wins (Giro 2020)
Exceptional recovery: survived back-to-back Pyrenean stages finishing last both days, then won sprint stage 18
Superior late-race positioning and bike handling in chaotic/messy sprint finales (2022 Giro evidence twice)
Heavily dependent on Groupama-FDJ leadout functioning; fails to feature when team support absent early
Demonstrates strong chase capability: rejoined after 20km mechanical to contest sprint
form_trajectory: positive: three sprint wins across 2022 Giro showing dominance over Cavendish/Bauhaus, continuing pattern of multiple wins per Grand Tour established in 2020
